findings.problem: Legacy 'Codex local review' terminology persists in schema instructions and risk-tier gating sections.
findings.evidence: openspec/schemas/ai-enforced-workflow/schema.yaml line 191 refers to 'Codex local review'; lines 230, 232, and 234 define risk tiers using 'Codex review' or 'Codex pre-review' instead of 'verifier-subagent review'.
findings.recommendation: Update all schema instructions to use 'verifier-subagent review' or 'verifier-subagent local review' consistently.

findings.problem: Apply and Repair skills continue to instruct the old 'Codex local review' terminology.
findings.evidence: .codex/skills/openspec-apply-change/SKILL.md line 108 ('Do not skip the Codex local review'); .codex/skills/openspec-repair-change/SKILL.md multiple locations (lines 19, 21, 47, 49, 97, 109) still use 'Codex local review' as the reviewer name.
findings.recommendation: Refactor apply and repair skill instructions to use 'verifier-subagent review' and match the new findings contract naming.

findings.problem: Artifact generation helper skills still explicitly teach the deprecated 'Codex local review first' sequence.
findings.evidence: .codex/skills/openspec-propose/SKILL.md:101, .codex/skills/openspec-ff-change/SKILL.md:92, and .codex/skills/openspec-continue-change/SKILL.md:116 still contain guardrails mentioning 'Codex local review first, Gemini independent verification second'.
findings.recommendation: Replace 'Codex local review first' with 'verifier-subagent review first' in all helper skills to ensure future artifacts are correctly aligned.

skipped_checks: Verification of platform-specific helper scripts (e.g., bin/*.sh, bin/*.ps1) as they were not explicitly provided in the file list for deep analysis.
final_assessment: The implementation correctly adopts the verifier-subagent orchestration structure, tier-gated Gemini policy, and normalized findings contract. However, coherence is degraded by extensive legacy terminology. Per-file instructions and skill guardrails still refer to 'Codex local review' or 'Codex pre-review' instead of the new 'verifier-subagent' role. These defects are accurately identified in the change's own implementation review (on-demand-implementation-subagent-review.json).
